---
title: "Patient-robust discovery of combinatorial cell-surface targets in prostate cancer"
subtitle: "AND-gate and NOT-gate marker pairs from single-cell data, benchmarked against PSMA-PSCA"
author: "William Guesdon"
date: now
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm"
abstract: |
  Single-antigen therapies against solid tumors are limited by on-target off-tumor toxicity, because
  few surface antigens are truly absent from all healthy tissue. Combinatorial (logic-gated) targeting
  requires two conditions before a cell is engaged, which can recover specificity. We scan a curated
  cell-surface panel in single-cell data for pairs that discriminate prostate cancer cells from healthy
  human cell types, under AND (both markers on the tumor) and NOT (an activator spared by a blocker on
  healthy cells) logic. Pairs are scored per patient and summarized across patients, so no high
  cell-count patient drives a result. The clinically validated PSMA-PSCA pair is recovered as a positive
  control: each marker alone carries a large extra-prostatic liability, and the AND gate collapses it.
  We report a Pareto frontier of surface-accessible pairs that improve per-patient coverage over
  PSMA-PSCA at comparable off-tissue risk, with Human Protein Atlas evidence qualifying targetability.
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: styles/report.css
    title-block-banner: false
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
    fig-align: center
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 5
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    fig-align: center
bibliography: references.bib
engine: jupyter
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  cache: false
---

```{python}
#| tags: [setup]
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib as mpl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

REPO = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "reports" else Path.cwd()
T = REPO / "results" / "tables"

def load(name):
    p = T / name
    return pd.read_csv(p) if p.exists() else None

manifest   = load("data_manifest.csv")
summary    = load("analysis_summary.csv").iloc[0]
panel      = load("surface_panel.csv")
singles    = load("singles_markers.csv")
pairs_and  = load("pairs_and.csv")
frontier   = load("surface_frontier.csv")
recovery   = load("psma_psca_recovery.csv")
negctrl    = load("negative_control.csv")
protein    = load("protein_evidence.csv")
nomination = load("nomination.csv").iloc[0]
nom_not    = load("nomination_not.csv")
sens       = load("threshold_sensitivity.csv")   # may be absent early
perpat     = load("nomination_per_patient.csv")
tissue_liab= load("pair_tissue_liability.csv")
dbl_rm     = load("doublet_removal.csv")
dbl_qc     = load("doublet_qc.csv")

# Headline scalars, all bound to committed tables.
N_TUMOR   = int(manifest.loc[manifest.dataset == "tumor_localised_pca", "n_cells"].iloc[0])
N_DONOR   = int(manifest.loc[manifest.dataset == "tumor_localised_pca", "n_donors"].iloc[0])
N_MAL     = int(summary.n_malignant_cells)
N_PAT     = int(summary.n_patients_scored)
N_BEN     = int(summary.n_benign_cells)
N_HEALTHY = int(summary.n_healthy_cells)
N_CT      = int(summary.n_healthy_celltypes)
N_TIS     = int(summary.n_healthy_tissues)
N_POP     = int(summary.n_healthy_populations)
N_GENE    = int(summary.n_panel_genes_scanned)
K         = int(summary.k_threshold)

# Report palette and matplotlib defaults.
PALETTE = {"ink": "#0A0F29", "blue": "#1E4FA3", "blue2": "#2E6FD0", "faint": "#dce5f2",
           "vermillion": "#c9542b", "grey": "#8c8c8c", "grid": "#e6e6e6", "green": "#2e7d5b"}
plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.dpi": 130, "savefig.dpi": 130, "font.size": 10, "font.family": "sans-serif",
    "axes.edgecolor": "#b8b8b2", "axes.linewidth": 0.8, "axes.grid": True,
    "grid.color": PALETTE["grid"], "grid.linewidth": 0.7, "axes.axisbelow": True,
    "axes.spines.top": False, "axes.spines.right": False,
})

def pair_row(df, a, b, ca="marker_a", cb="marker_b"):
    m = (((df[ca] == a) & (df[cb] == b)) | ((df[ca] == b) & (df[cb] == a)))
    return df[m].iloc[0]
```

# Background

Most targeted therapies against solid tumors fail on safety, not potency. A single tumor antigen is
rarely absent from every healthy tissue, so single-target CAR-T, T-cell engagers, and antibody-drug
conjugates carry on-target off-tumor toxicity. Combinatorial, logic-gated targeting requires two
conditions before a cell is engaged and can recover the missing specificity [@kloss2013combinatorial;
@roybal2016precision; @cho2018universal]. Prostate adenocarcinoma is the natural test bed: the
PSMA (FOLH1) x PSCA pair is a preclinically validated AND-gate split-signal CAR [@kloss2013combinatorial],
so an unsupervised scan can be checked against a known answer, and STEAP1 anchors a second axis with a
STEAP1 x CD3 T-cell engager (xaluritamig) in trials [@kelly2024xaluritamig].

Computational logic-gated antigen discovery is not itself new: pan-cancer single-cell pair scans
[@kwon2023singlecell] and a breast-cancer AND/OR/NOT circuit search [@madan2025logicar] precede this
work. Our contribution is the combination of (i) per-patient scoring rather than pooled cells,
(ii) a matched benign-prostate control that tests tumor specificity in the same samples, and
(iii) recovery of the PSMA-PSCA benchmark before any new pair is nominated.

# Data

```{python}
Markdown(f"""
- **Tumor discovery cohort** — {N_TUMOR:,} cells from **{N_DONOR} patients** with localised, hormone
  therapy-naive prostate adenocarcinoma [@localisedpca2024]. Malignant identity uses the authors'
  copy-number and signature-based annotation: **{N_MAL:,} malignant cells** (scDblFinder singlets;
  see Methods), with **{N_BEN:,} adjacent-benign / normal prostate epithelial cells** retained as a
  matched same-patient control.
- **Healthy reference** — Tabula Sapiens [@tabulasapiens2022], pulled through the CELLxGENE Census with a
  server-side subset to the panel genes: **{N_HEALTHY:,} cells**, **{N_CT} cell types** across **{N_TIS}
  tissues** ({N_POP} tissue-by-cell-type populations scored). This is the safety denominator.
- **Protein evidence** — Human Protein Atlas subcellular localization and normal-tissue RNA
  [@uhlen2015hpa], used to qualify surface accessibility and cross-check normal-tissue liabilities.
- **Panel** — {N_GENE} curated cell-surface markers scanned (see Methods); secreted markers (KLK3/PSA,
  KLK2, ACPP) are kept only as comparators.
""")
```

# Methods

```{python}
if dbl_rm is not None:
    d = dbl_rm.iloc[0]
    maxd = float(np.abs(dbl_qc["delta"]).max()) if dbl_qc is not None else float("nan")
    display(Markdown(
        f"- **Doublet removal (primary input).** scDblFinder, run per patient in a Bioconductor "
        f"container, flagged **{d['doublet_rate']*100:.1f}%** of tumor cells as doublets "
        f"({d['malignant_doublet_rate']*100:.1f}% of malignant cells); these are removed before "
        f"scoring, so a two-cells-as-one capture cannot fake AND co-expression (Rule 8). Re-scoring "
        f"on the {int(d['n_cells_singlet']):,} retained single cells moves the key pairs' AND coverage "
        f"by at most {maxd*100:.1f} percentage points, so the finding is not a doublet artifact."
    ))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-doublet-qc
#| fig-cap: "Doublet robustness. Median per-patient AND coverage of the key pairs computed on all malignant cells (grey) versus the scDblFinder singlets actually used for scoring (blue). The bars are near-identical and the change (annotated, percentage points) is at most one point, so the co-expression signal is not an artifact of two cells captured as one; removing doublets nudges the nominated pairs slightly upward."
if dbl_qc is not None:
    q = dbl_qc.reset_index(drop=True)
    labels = [p.replace("FOLH1", "PSMA").replace("+", " x ") for p in q["pair"]]
    x = np.arange(len(q)); w = 0.38
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3.4))
    ax.bar(x - w/2, q["and_coverage_all"], width=w, color=PALETTE["grey"], label="all malignant cells")
    ax.bar(x + w/2, q["and_coverage_singlets"], width=w, color=PALETTE["blue"], label="singlets (scored)")
    for i, r in q.iterrows():
        top = max(r["and_coverage_all"], r["and_coverage_singlets"])
        ax.text(i, top + 0.02, f"{r['delta']*100:+.1f} pp", ha="center", fontsize=8, color=PALETTE["ink"])
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(labels); ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
    ax.set_ylabel("AND coverage (median across patients)")
    ax.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=8, loc="upper left")
    plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

- **Positivity** is a raw count of at least `{python} K` per cell (detection), never a single
  `count > 0` shortcut baked into the summary; the threshold is swept for sensitivity (below).
- **Per-patient scoring (never pooled).** For markers A, B and each patient's malignant cells,
  AND coverage is `P(A+ and B+ | malignant)` computed within that patient, then summarized across
  patients by the lower decile (Q0.10, the robustness floor) and the median. A patient with thousands of
  cells cannot dominate a pair's score.
- **Three references per pair.** (1) malignant coverage; (2) **matched benign prostate** from the same
  cohort, so a pair expressed across all prostate cells is penalised, not just one specific to the tumor;
  (3) **healthy liability** as the worst-case co-positive fraction over Tabula Sapiens populations,
  reported both across all organs and **excluding the prostate** (the target organ is dispensable; every
  other organ is the dose-limiting risk).
- **Gate algebra.** For each group a single matrix product over the panel positivity matrix yields AND,
  NOT (`A+ and B-`) and OR counts for every pair at once.
- **Ranking.** Pairs are placed on a Pareto frontier of per-patient coverage floor (higher better)
  against worst extra-prostatic liability (lower better). Only surface-accessible markers are
  nominatable.
- **Reproducibility.** Every number below is read from a committed table in `results/tables/`; the report
  redraws its figures from those tables with no network or model call.

# Results

## Single surface antigens are not safe alone

```{python}
#| label: fig-singles
#| fig-cap: "Single surface antigens: per-patient malignant coverage (median) against the worst-case extra-prostatic healthy liability. Every strong single antigen carries a large off-tissue liability; the recovered worst tissues match known biology (FOLH1/PSMA to duodenum, PSCA to bladder urothelium)."
keys = ["FOLH1","PSCA","STEAP1","STEAP2","STEAP4","TMPRSS2","HPN","TACSTD2","CD276","DLL3"]
s = singles.set_index("marker").loc[[k for k in keys if k in singles.marker.values]]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
y = np.arange(len(s))
ax.barh(y-0.2, s["tumor_median"], height=0.4, color=PALETTE["blue"], label="malignant coverage (median)")
ax.barh(y+0.2, s["worst_healthy_xprostate"], height=0.4, color=PALETTE["vermillion"], label="worst extra-prostatic liability")
for i,(m,r) in enumerate(s.iterrows()):
    ax.text(r["worst_healthy_xprostate"]+0.01, i+0.2, str(r["worst_xp_celltype"])[:26], va="center", fontsize=7, color=PALETTE["grey"])
ax.set_yticks(y); ax.set_yticklabels([f"{m} ({s.loc[m,'alt_name']})" if 'alt_name' in s.columns else m for m in s.index])
ax.invert_yaxis(); ax.set_xlim(0,1.15); ax.set_xlabel("fraction of cells positive"); ax.legend(loc="lower right", fontsize=8, frameon=False)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

- Each strong single antigen is broadly positive on the tumor but also on at least one healthy cell
  type: the scan independently recovers PSMA's duodenal and PSCA's bladder-urothelial expression, so the
  liability calls are trustworthy.

## The AND gate recovers PSMA-PSCA and collapses its single-agent liabilities

```{python}
#| label: fig-recovery
#| fig-cap: "Positive control. PSMA (FOLH1) and PSCA are each broadly expressed off the prostate (duodenum, bladder), but requiring both at once (AND) collapses the worst extra-prostatic liability roughly sevenfold, recovering the clinical rationale for the split-signal PSMA x PSCA CAR."
r = recovery.set_index("entity")
order = list(r.index)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3.6))
colors = [PALETTE["vermillion"], PALETTE["vermillion"], PALETTE["green"]]
bars = ax.bar(order, r["worst_healthy_xprostate"], color=colors, width=0.6)
for b,(e,row) in zip(bars, r.iterrows()):
    ax.text(b.get_x()+b.get_width()/2, row["worst_healthy_xprostate"]+0.02, f"{row['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}\n{str(row['worst_xp_celltype'])[:18]}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=7.5)
ax.set_ylim(0,1.05); ax.set_ylabel("worst extra-prostatic liability"); ax.set_xlabel("")
plt.xticks(rotation=8); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
pc = pair_row(pairs_and, "FOLH1", "PSCA")
sa = singles.set_index("marker")
red = max(sa.loc["FOLH1","worst_healthy_xprostate"], sa.loc["PSCA","worst_healthy_xprostate"]) - pc["worst_healthy_xprostate"]
frac_worse = float(negctrl.loc[negctrl.row=="summary","frac_random_pairs_worse_than_psma_psca"].iloc[0])
Markdown(f"""
- **Recovered.** PSMA alone reaches **{sa.loc['FOLH1','worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** in duodenal
  enterocytes and PSCA alone **{sa.loc['PSCA','worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** in bladder urothelium;
  the **PSMA and PSCA** gate drops the worst extra-prostatic liability to
  **{pc['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** (a reduction of {red:.2f}).
- **Above the random baseline (Rule 7).** {frac_worse*100:.0f}% of random surface pairs score worse than
  PSMA-PSCA on extra-prostatic selectivity, and PSMA-PSCA sits on the surface-accessible Pareto frontier.
- Its low raw coverage ({pc['tumor_median']:.2f} median) reflects PSCA transcript dropout, not a biology
  failure; this is the motivation to look for higher-coverage pairs of equal safety.
""")
```

## A Pareto frontier of surface pairs improves on PSMA-PSCA

```{python}
#| label: fig-pareto
#| fig-cap: "Surface-accessible AND pairs: per-patient coverage floor (Q0.10) against worst extra-prostatic liability. Upper-left is better. Frontier pairs are filled; the PSMA-PSCA positive control and the two co-lead nominations are labelled. Secreted markers and intracellular prostein are excluded as non-targetable."
f = frontier.copy()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.5, 5))
off = f[~f.on_frontier]; on = f[f.on_frontier]
ax.scatter(off["worst_healthy_xprostate"], off["tumor_q10"], s=22, color=PALETTE["grey"], alpha=0.5, label="surface pair")
ax.scatter(on["worst_healthy_xprostate"], on["tumor_q10"], s=48, color=PALETTE["blue"], label="Pareto frontier", zorder=3)
labels = {("FOLH1","PSCA"):"PSMA x PSCA (control)", ("FOLH1","STEAP1"):"PSMA x STEAP1", ("STEAP1","HPN"):"STEAP1 x HPN"}
for (a,b),txt in labels.items():
    row = pair_row(f, a, b)
    ax.scatter(row["worst_healthy_xprostate"], row["tumor_q10"], s=70, facecolor="none", edgecolor=PALETTE["vermillion"], linewidth=1.6, zorder=4)
    ax.annotate(txt, (row["worst_healthy_xprostate"], row["tumor_q10"]), textcoords="offset points", xytext=(8,4), fontsize=8.5, color=PALETTE["ink"])
ax.set_xlabel("worst extra-prostatic liability  (lower is safer)")
ax.set_ylabel("per-patient coverage floor  Q0.10  (higher is better)")
ax.set_xlim(-0.02, max(0.6, f["worst_healthy_xprostate"].max()*1.05)); ax.set_ylim(-0.02, 1.0)
ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=8, frameon=False); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
fold = float(nomination["coverage_fold_vs_psma_psca"])
sthpn = pair_row(pairs_and, "STEAP1", "HPN")
Markdown(f"""
Two surface-accessible co-leads improve on the control (both recovered as safe, higher-coverage pairs):

- **PSMA x STEAP1 — translatable lead.** Both antigens have clinical-grade binders. Per-patient coverage
  floor **Q0.10 = {nomination['tumor_q10']:.2f}**, median **{nomination['tumor_median']:.2f}**
  (**{fold:.1f}x** the PSMA-PSCA median), worst extra-prostatic liability
  **{nomination['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** in {nomination['worst_xp_celltype']}, versus
  {nomination['single_a_worst_xp']:.2f} / {nomination['single_b_worst_xp']:.2f} for the single antigens.
- **STEAP1 x HPN — Pareto-optimal lead.** Coverage floor **Q0.10 = {sthpn['tumor_q10']:.2f}**, median
  **{sthpn['tumor_median']:.2f}**, worst extra-prostatic liability **{sthpn['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}**
  ({sthpn['worst_xp_celltype']}) — the lowest off-tissue risk among high-coverage pairs.
- **Tumor-specific, not pan-prostate.** Both leads separate malignant from matched benign prostate
  (malignant-minus-benign coverage {nomination['malignant_vs_benign']:.2f} for PSMA x STEAP1), so the
  gate marks cancer rather than the whole gland.
""")
```

## Robustness: per patient and across positivity thresholds

```{python}
#| label: fig-perpatient
#| fig-cap: "Per-patient AND coverage of the malignant compartment for the positive control and the two co-lead nominations. Each point is one patient; the bar is the median. PSMA x STEAP1 and STEAP1 x HPN cover the tumor in every scored patient, while PSMA x PSCA is uniformly low (PSCA transcript dropout)."
pp = perpat.copy()
order = ["FOLH1 + PSCA", "FOLH1 + STEAP1", "STEAP1 + HPN"]
pp = pp[pp.pair.isin(order)]
colors = {"FOLH1 + PSCA": PALETTE["grey"], "FOLH1 + STEAP1": PALETTE["blue"], "STEAP1 + HPN": PALETTE["green"]}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.5, 4))
rng = np.random.default_rng(1)
for i, name in enumerate(order):
    v = pp.loc[pp.pair == name, "coverage"].to_numpy()
    x = i + (rng.random(len(v)) - 0.5) * 0.28
    ax.scatter(x, v, s=26, color=colors[name], alpha=0.75, edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.4, zorder=3)
    ax.hlines(np.median(v), i-0.28, i+0.28, color=PALETTE["ink"], linewidth=2, zorder=4)
ax.set_xticks(range(len(order))); ax.set_xticklabels([o.replace("FOLH1","PSMA") for o in order])
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.0); ax.set_ylabel("per-patient malignant AND coverage")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-sensitivity
#| fig-cap: "Positivity-threshold sensitivity. As the per-cell call is tightened from >=1 to >=3 counts, coverage falls modestly but the worst extra-prostatic liability also falls: the nominated pairs stay well covered and become safer, whereas PSMA x PSCA loses coverage. Positivity is never a single count>0 call."
if sens is not None:
    keys = ["FOLH1 + PSCA", "FOLH1 + STEAP1", "STEAP1 + HPN"]
    cmap = {"FOLH1 + PSCA": PALETTE["grey"], "FOLH1 + STEAP1": PALETTE["blue"], "STEAP1 + HPN": PALETTE["green"]}
    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 3.6))
    for name in keys:
        s = sens[sens.pair == name].sort_values("k")
        lab = name.replace("FOLH1","PSMA")
        ax1.plot(s.k, s.tumor_median, "-o", color=cmap[name], label=lab, markersize=5)
        ax2.plot(s.k, s.worst_healthy_xprostate, "-o", color=cmap[name], label=lab, markersize=5)
    ax1.set_xticks([1,2,3]); ax2.set_xticks([1,2,3])
    ax1.set_xlabel("positivity threshold (min counts)"); ax1.set_ylabel("malignant coverage (median)")
    ax2.set_xlabel("positivity threshold (min counts)"); ax2.set_ylabel("worst extra-prostatic liability")
    ax1.set_ylim(0,1); ax2.set_ylim(0, None); ax1.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
    plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
else:
    display(Markdown("_Threshold-sensitivity table not yet generated._"))
```

- Both nominations survive threshold tightening: coverage decreases gradually while the worst
  extra-prostatic liability falls, so the safety claim is not an artifact of the loosest positivity call.

```{python}
if tissue_liab is not None:
    tl = tissue_liab.copy()
    tl = tl[tl.tissue.str.lower() != "prostate gland"].head(6)
    display(Markdown("Residual off-tumor liabilities of PSMA x STEAP1 are named, not hidden. The highest "
             "extra-prostatic healthy populations (AND co-positive fraction):\n\n" +
             tl[["tissue","cell_type","coverage","n_cells"]].round(3).to_markdown(index=False)))
```

## Exploratory NOT gates

```{python}
cols = ["activator","blocker","tumor_q10","tumor_median","worst_healthy_xprostate","worst_xp_celltype"]
Markdown("NOT gates (activator on the tumor, blocker sparing a normal tissue) are reported as "
         "exploratory: the blocker-negative call depends on trusting scRNA dropout. Top surface "
         "activator / normal-tissue blocker candidates:\n\n" +
         nom_not[cols].head(5).round(3).to_markdown(index=False))
```

# Limitations

- **Transcript is not targetability.** scRNA measures mRNA, not surface antigen density, epitope
  accessibility, shedding, or internalization. HPA localization is used to exclude secreted markers and
  intracellular prostein, but same-cell surface co-expression needs CITE-seq, multiplex IHC, or dual
  flow to confirm.
- **NOT-gate calls trust B-negatives**, which are dropout-sensitive; they are exploratory.
- **One cohort, localised disease.** The discovery cohort is hormone-naive localised prostate cancer;
  replication in an independent and a castration-resistant / neuroendocrine cohort is future work.
- **Malignant labels are the authors' CNV/signature calls**, adopted rather than re-derived here.
- **PSCA transcript dropout** caps PSMA-PSCA coverage; the pair is validated on safety, not coverage.

# Reproducibility

The pipeline runs from a clean clone with `uv`; every table in `results/tables/` is regenerated by the
numbered scripts in `scripts/`, and this report redraws its figures from those tables. See the README for
the exact command sequence.

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
